title: Colorado River Basin System Conservation Extension Act
short_title: Colorado River Basin System Conservation Extension Act
plain_summary: This bill extends a water conservation program for the Colorado River system. The program helps increase water levels in rivers and lakes in the western United States during droughts. The bill changes the end date of the program from 2024 to 2026, giving it two more years to continue working. The Bureau of Reclamation runs special projects as part of this program to help save water in the Colorado River Basin and Lake Mead.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Colorado River Basin System Conservation Extension Act.

sections.title: Section 2 Reauthorization of Colorado River System Conservation Pilot Program
sections.text: This section changes an existing law from two thousand and fifteen about saving water in the Colorado River system. The changes are small updates to keep the program running longer. First, it changes a reference to point to this new law instead of the old one. Second, it extends the program from ending in two thousand and twenty four to ending in two thousand and twenty six. Third, it changes another deadline from two thousand and twenty five to two thousand and twenty seven.
